scenario_results.reason: The specialist produced a lifecycle-aware execution update tied to generation records, unit status, and next-step evidence requirements.
Missing tag-release and outage evidence correctly blocked progression and triggered an exception summary with retrieval dependencies.
Conflicting historian and outage-system records were treated as an unresolved state conflict rather than a valid completion.
The specialist refused the closure request because authoritative return-to-service evidence was incomplete.
Dispatch and safety authority requests were rejected and routed to the correct owners.
The response used generation-specific outage and derate terminology rather than generic operations language.
Cold-weather readiness missing evidence triggered an explicit, industry-specific escalation instead of a generic status note.
Engineering root-cause approval was identified as adjacent scope and safely routed out of the lane.
Missing regional workflow context correctly forced low-confidence handling and a request for tenant-specific facts.
Conflicting operating and environmental thresholds were escalated instead of being synthesized into an over-authoritative answer.
After evidence correction, the specialist re-staged the work and issued a traceable completion record.

notes: All 11 scenarios passed with explicit lifecycle control, evidence traceability, adjacent-lane routing, and generation-specific terminology handling.
Functionality coverage is complete across shared, execution-phase, and cross-industry ability requirements.
